agent_runs.outcome: validation_rejected (1 error: output_mapping constant field without 'logic')
fixed logic field; then H5 hygiene rejected (5 compound [VERIFIED: CL-x + path] markers)
split compound markers; submitted (28 claims, 33 deps)
REVERT (4 dependency citations mis-attributed/off-by-one rated not-found; 9 claims partially_supported; independently re-counted all count claims)
fixed 4 dep citations (including correcting the judge's own off-by-one hint), fixed 10 partials; submitted (27 claims, 33 deps)
REVERT (CL-002 count 554 vs judge's 558: judge re-counted but missed the 4 comment-only hits the author had excluded)
verified the 4 disputed lines are comments char-by-char; reformulated CL-002 to the grep-reproducible raw count (558) with the comment nuance in prose; submitted
ACCEPT (60/60 coverage: 26 supported, 1 partially_supported medium on a pattern claim, 33/33 deps confirmed)

notes: Deepest analysis of all configs: the author identified the file as a vendored abapmerge build (v1.133.0, abapmerge 0.16.8), correctly excluded local zcl_abapgit_* classes as self-dependencies, and found the 65 collision-renamed classes.
The sonnet judge is far stricter than haiku: it independently re-ran every whole-file count and caught 4 off-by-one/mis-attributed dependency citations that a weaker judge would have confirmed.

Round-2 REVERT was a judge false positive on comment detection (554 vs 558 CALL FUNCTION): the author proved the 4 disputed hits are full-line comments. The fix that survived was reformulating the claim to the grep-reproducible number - exact 'active statement' counts on a 153k-line file are razor-edge claims.
Author validation retries were format-related (output_mapping schema, compound confidence markers), not content-related.

agent_runs.outcome: submitted (12 gaps: 5 auto-answered, 7 open incl. PURPOSE - interpreted PURPOSE as deployment-specific, refused to close it from generic docs)
questionnaire (7 questions) -> owner's session statement captured via capture-answer: 5 gaps answered (PURPOSE, TRIGGER, ACTOR, CONFIG anomaly, online-mode)
submitted first try (16 FUN + 8 PRC claims) - self-validated against functional_io before returning
ACCEPT (16/16: 11 supported, 5 partially_supported incl. one genuine overreach finding on FUN-008)
ACCEPT (8/8: 6 supported, 2 rule-capped partials; explicitly rejected a false-contradiction candidate)

notes: The sonnet researcher was the most skeptical of all: it refused to close PURPOSE from the generic abapGit docs and demanded the deployment-specific answer - which forced the full Phase 3 loop (questionnaire -> owner answer -> capture-answer). More faithful to the L2 design, at the cost of one human turnaround.

It also produced the benchmark's only multi-source auto-closure: proving no customer-exit includes exist by cross-referencing the TADIR export with dependency-derived stubs, and flagged the Z-package-vs-$-package [ANOMALY].
The functional author self-validated with the pipeline's own validators before returning - zero fix passes (vs 3 for haiku).
